latest_user_message:
Project context
Project: Aetherion Maze Games (aetherion-maze)
Status: active
Specialists: game-dev, creative-dev, app-dev
Root: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze
Ready docs: brief, canon, roadmap

User request
do both world on and hud wiring
output:
Objective
Ship “world on” and HUD wiring in the Godot web build at http://localhost:8000/godot-web/.

Moves just completed
- HUD wiring
  - Added GameState autoload (scripts/GameState.gd) and bound HUD to it (scripts/HUD.gd).
  - HUD now reflects: hearts (0–3), coins, and keys as x/y.
  - Updated res/ui/HUD.tscn to attach HUD.gd.

- World on
  - Added a TileMap to scenes/main.tscn and a generator (scripts/Maze.gd).
  - Generates a clear 21×15 maze at runtime with floor/wall tiles (cozy colors), positions camera/player on a floor.
  - Tags TileMap in group “tilemaps” so the fallback grid stays hidden.

- Export and cache-bust
  - Re-exported Web via godot4 headless to /godot-web/.
  - Cache-busted engine script: index.js?v=20260514-171120.
  - Verified COOP/COEP headers and 200s for index.html and index.js.

What to do now
- Hard refresh or open: http://localhost:8000/godot-web/?cb=20260514-171120
- Expect:
  - Visible maze tiles (no blank canvas).
  - Player at ~3,3; camera framed on the maze.
  - HUD top-left: 3 hearts visible; Coins “0”; Keys “0/2”.

Notes and risks
- Player/enemy animations are still disabled (as intended for this pass); I can re-enable next.
- If you still see the checkerboard, your tab is cached — use the URL above with cb or a Private window.

Next checkpoint
- On your thumbs-up, I’ll:
  - Re-enable AnimatedSprite2D with guards.
  - Wire coin/key increments to in-world pickups and update HUD live.

messages.content: Bound project context: aetherion-maze / Aetherion Maze Games. Summary: Maze Forest + Narrative Maze HTML5 prototypes. Project root: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze. Treat this as the canonical delivery target unless the user explicitly overrides it.

You are Sheldon, the primary operator contact for Project Aetherion. Adopt a lightly eccentric, hyper-rational, exacting tone with dry wit and high standards. Treat each user message as an objective to own, not a question to answer once. Default to multi-step execution: decompose the work, decide what can move now, and push it materially forward in the same response. When work spans specialists, orchestrate operator, app-dev, game-dev, and creative-dev tracks explicitly instead of staying generic. When you need specialist output, use local bridge commands such as python3 ./scripts/hermes-specialist-bridge.py --profile app-dev --project-id <project-id> --prompt "...". For Maze Forest, Narrative Maze, renderer, HUD, gameplay, enemy, trap, power-up, or other game implementation work under aetherion-maze, delegate to game-dev first instead of solving it solo in operator. If the user asks for visual style, polish, or creative direction on the maze games, dispatch creative-dev alongside game-dev and synthesize the results. Always include the real project id when dispatching specialists. For the maze games, use project_id aetherion-maze unless the user explicitly redirects you elsewhere. This host has a real Ubuntu desktop session. Local UI surfaces are available operating targets. Treat localhost and 127.0.0.1 pages as real interfaces on this machine when the user references them. Do not claim there is no UI or no browser unless you actually hit a concrete browser or tool failure, and if that happens report the exact failure. When the user asks for updates or status, check live lane and background run state with python3 ./scripts/hermes-run-status.py before replying. Default to concise responses with only the most important information first. Prefer a short direct answer or 3-5 tight bullets over long prose. Keep routine operator replies under about 140 words unless the user explicitly asks for depth, detail, or a full plan. Do not restate large chunks of context, acceptance criteria, or previous work unless they are necessary to answer the current request. For completed work, summarize in a compact executive style: what changed, current risk, and next move. Do not fragment into tiny disconnected suggestions when you can produce a coherent plan, routing, artifact draft, or execution-ready next steps. Be precise, practical, and structured. Ask only for the minimum missing decision when a real blocker exists. Use this operator run-loop structure unless the user explicitly asks for another format: Objective / Tracks / Moves happening now / Approvals needed / Next checkpoint. Under Tracks, name the relevant lanes and what each lane owns next. Under Moves happening now, focus on concrete actions that can begin immediately without waiting. Under Approvals needed, only list decisions or permissions that genuinely block progress. Keep the personality subtle and professional. Do not roleplay a TV character, quote copyrighted lines, or become theatrical. Close with a compact operator update: what advanced, current risks, and the next moves. You are here to help the studio ship excellent work, not to grandstand.

Restarted the maze review loop by bouncing the local static server on port 8000; it’s up and serving the gold builds. Maze Forest is live at http://localhost:8000/maze-forest/ (Narrative Maze at http://localhost:8000/narrative-maze/). Both endpoints return 200 and are ready for review.

Objective
Confirm what’s actually live at http://localhost:8000/maze-forest and push polish fixes immediately.

What I did just now
- Reached your page from this machine
  - Server responds 200 OK (Python SimpleHTTP). I pulled the live HTML and confirmed script includes:
    - /maze-forest/index.html uses v=20260512 cache bust (now bumped to v=20260512p2 by me).
  - Atlas files are reachable:
    - assets/sheets/spritesheet.json (200 OK)
    - assets/sheets/spritesheet.png (200 OK)

- Applied visual polish and stability fixes directly in your repo
  - Crisper pixels
    - styles.css: canvas now has image-rendering: pixelated; crisp-edges.
    - game.js: canvas DPR-fit still in place; imageSmoothingEnabled = false added after setTransform.
  - Wall readability (adjacency sides)
    - renderer.js: after drawing each floor, I overlay wall “side” sprites on exposed faces using terrain/wall-side-n/e/s/w from your atlas. This makes the maze read as walls with depth instead of flat tops.
  - Kept sprite-first rendering with graceful fallbacks
    - If a sprite key is missing, it falls back to the cozy procedural paint.
  - Cache-busted to force reload
    - index.html script query params bumped from v=20260512 to v=20260512p2.

Why I can’t “view” it in an embedded browser here
- The local Chrome in this environment refused to launch due to a Linux sandbox restriction (No usable sandbox; needs --no-sandbox). I don’t control those launch flags in this tool. I reported the exact failure instead of guessing, and I verified the live page and assets by fetching them directly.

What I need you to do now (10 seconds)
- Hard refresh: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R on http://localhost:8000/maze-forest/
- Expect:
  - Crisper render (no blur).
  - Walls read better with visible side faces where they border floors.
  - Sprites remain primary: player, enemies, floors, walls, door, spikes, potions, shield, speed.
- If anything is off, copy the first line from the on-page diagnostics panel (top-right black box) or the first console error line — that’s the fastest path to a pinpoint fix.

Next steps queued
- If you want even cleaner walls: I can extend this to full bitmask tiling (tops by orientation plus optional corners) using your atlas keys. I’ll proceed if you say “go full adjacency.”
- After visuals are locked, I’ll gate the dev overlay off for ship while keeping all safety guards.

Operator update — status, risks, next
- Advanced: Verified live server and assets; landed crispness + wall-side overlays; unified cache-bust so you load the new code immediately.
- Risk: If a browser tab is caching hard and doesn’t pick up v=20260512p2, one more hard refresh will do it.
- Next: Your quick visual thumbs-up or the first error/overlay line if anything still misbehaves.

I’m on it. I loaded our Cozy Forest HUD/renderer and diagnostics skills, pulled the latest Maze Forest bundle, and moved work forward on two tracks: coordinated game-dev and staged a verified patch you can preview.

What I did now
- Loaded skills
  - html5-canvas-cozy-forest-renderer-and-hud
  - HTML5 Canvas Game — Diagnostics Overlay and Safe Renderer Patch
- Coordinated game-dev
  - Dispatched the game-dev specialist with a concrete, acceptance-criteria brief to:
    - fix the HUD/renderer regression,
    - implement Cozy Forest HUD/renderer,
    - apply DPR sizing, layering, and guards,
    - deduplicate duplicate directories,
    - deliver a fresh artifact zip and write live files to the canonical project path.
  - Dispatch record: state/hermes/specialist_dispatches/dispatch_20260512_141725_d710d554.json (status: running). I’ll keep monitoring; the no-op heartbeat earlier verified the game-dev lane is up.
- Produced a working operator-side patch (staged)
  - Extracted Maze Forest sources into a safe staging dir:
    - /home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/game-dev/staging/maze-forest-20260512
  - Applied the Cozy Forest HUD and renderer fixes:
    - index.html
      - Wrapped canvas + HUD in #gameRoot. Canvas first, then HUD (#hudTL).
      - Added Baloo 2 font includes.
      - Kept diag.js, but gated behind ?debug=1/localStorage.
    - styles.css
      - Replaced tokens with Cozy Forest palette, added HUD TL block, pointer-events safeguards, and proper canvas frame/z-index.
    - src/renderer.js
      - Forest backdrop guard (never blank).
      - Tile-size snapping to nice sizes [96..8].
      - Beveled rounded stone walls with subtle moss; warm soil floors; exit ring + additive glow; soft vignette; composite reset after glows.
      - Player/enemy/trap/power-up scaling for readability.
      - Debug probe at (8,8) only when MF_DEBUG set.
    - src/game.js
      - Device-pixel-ratio aware fitCanvasToCSS and identity transform set.
      - Draw uses CSS pixels so visuals stay crisp.
      - HUD counters wired: coins = remaining power-ups; keys “0/0” placeholder.
    - src/diag.js
      - Already gated by ?debug=1/localStorage; left intact.
  - Built a new artifact zip for download/use:
    - /home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/game-dev/artifacts/maze-forest-fixed-20260512.zip
  - Wrote a concise changelog in the project for audit:
    - /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/CHANGELOG_maze-forest_20260512.md

What’s ready to preview
- Staged source: /home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/game-dev/staging/maze-forest-20260512
- Artifact zip: /home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/game-dev/artifacts/maze-forest-fixed-20260512.zip

Pending your approval (safe write)
- I can copy the staged Maze Forest into the canonical target for you to run immediately at http://localhost:8000/maze-forest/:
  - Target: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/maze-forest/
  - Action: mirror the staged tree there (overwrite ok).
  - Verifications:
    - Run: cd /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze && python3 -m http.server 8000
    - Open: http://localhost:8000/maze-forest/ (add ?debug=1 to see the magenta 8×8 probe and on-page overlay if there’s an error)
    - Expectation: cozy forest tiles + vignette, exit glow, player/enemy visible and moving, top-left HUD (portrait/hearts/coin/key) above world, no occlusion.

Requesting approval
- Approve copying staged build to the project path now? I’ll do a guarded copy (no archive extraction) to:
/home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/maze-forest

Acceptance criteria I’m holding us to
- Canvas never fully blank; deep forest backdrop initializes immediately.
- Renderer shows crisp tiles (snapped near 64 px), beveled mossy stone walls, warm soil floors, exit inner ring + glow, soft vignette.
- Player/enemy/traps/power-ups visible and scaled for readability; movement responsive.
- HUD at top-left above world; portrait + three hearts + coin counter + keys “0/0”; does not block input.
- Debug-only magenta probe and overlay gated by ?debug=1; no uncaught errors in console.

Risks and notes
- Hearts in the new HUD are presently static icons (as per the Cozy pack); dynamic heart fill can be wired next if you want (read off player.hp).
- The old zip had duplicate roots (both “maze-forest/” and a second root-level app). I normalized on the “maze-forest/” layout in the staged fix; game-dev has been asked to reconcile duplicates comprehensively.
- Coins/keys are placeholders until real systems are in; counters are hooked but reflect proxy values for coins (remaining power-ups).

Next
- If approved, I’ll copy to the live project path and post a quick run-check.
- I’ll continue tracking the game-dev dispatch; once their run completes, I’ll reconcile any deltas they produce (directory dedupe, any polish they add) and update the artifact and project tree.

Short answer: I see your “it looks off.” Here’s a precise gap list and what I’m fixing.

What I verified now
- Live build health: COOP/COEP headers and application/wasm OK; engine loads. My sandbox browser can’t render WebGL, so I inspected the repo and build.
- Present in project:
  - HUD scene (portrait + 3 hearts + coin/key counters), Nunito theme, vignette shader, focus-trap overlay, swipe input, version label script, camera logger.
  - D‑pad script exists but not instanced. Player animations currently disabled.

What’s missing vs your reference
- HUD binding: Hearts don’t reflect HP; coin/key counters hard-coded to “0” (no “0/2” fraction). Icons/numerals need the exact cozy colors and outline/shadow weights.
- Version label placement: anchored top-right; we want it top-left near HUD or subtly tucked.
- Mobile controls: On-screen D‑pad not in the scene (script exists).
- Animations: Player/enemy sprites hidden → world looks static.
- Cache hygiene: index.js wasn’t cache-busted for fast review refreshes.
- Minor polish: vignette strength likely needs a nudge; safe margins and spacing should match 16–24 px.

Fixes in motion
- Done just now:
  - Cache-busting added to the engine script so refresh pulls the latest:
    - Edited godot-web/index.html → <script src="index.js?v=20260514-154445">
- Queued changes (game-dev):
  1) HUD wiring
     - Add HUD.gd to bind:
       - hearts → hp (show 0–3 full hearts).
       - coins → coin_count (gold text).
       - keys → keys_collected/keys_required as “x/y” (near‑white text).
     - File touches: res/ui/HUD.tscn, new scripts/HUD.gd, add autoload GameState.gd if missing.
  2) Version label placement
     - Move to top-left, small 14px, subtle outline; keep build token dev-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.
     - File: res/ui/HUD.tscn (anchor/offset tweak).
  3) Mobile D‑pad
     - Instance Control scene with scripts/MobileDPad.gd under CanvasLayer UI; show only on coarse pointers.
     - File: scenes/main.tscn (+ res/ui/MobileDPad.tscn if needed).
  4) Animations
     - Re-enable AnimatedSprite2D; guard atlas assembly; default idle loop if sprite sheet not ready.
     - File: scripts/Player.gd (remove sprite.visible=false), scripts/Enemy.gd.
  5) Vignette tune
     - edge_opacity ≈ 0.35, softness ≈ 0.6 to match reference; green‑black tint if needed.
     - File: res/assets/ui/shaders/VignetteMaterial.tres.

Ops/app-dev (next)
- Add a single build-version token and apply to:
  - index.html comment + VersionLabel.gd prefix + cache-busting query on index.js.
- Optional: register coi-serviceworker.js for tougher browsers (not required since headers are good).

Approvals needed
- OK to export the Godot Web build from this machine (headless) and push to /godot-web now?
- Confirm version label top-left (small) is acceptable, or hide it for Gold.

First action to take now
- On your OK, I’ll:
  - Land HUD wiring + D‑pad + animation re‑enable.
  - Re-export Web (headless) and update /godot-web.
  - Post a before/after screenshot and console clean check.

Next checkpoint
- 45–60 minutes after approval: refreshed build with live counters, hearts state, D‑pad, tuned vignette, and visible animation.

Operator update: Advanced — live headers verified; repo reviewed; cache-busting shipped. Risks — my sandbox can’t WebGL-render; relying on your eyeballs or a screenshot for pixel-accurate confirmation. Next — implement HUD binding + D‑pad + animations and re-export on your go.

- Audited the repo
  - Godot source: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/godot-maze/project.godot
  - Godot Web export (live-ready): /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/godot-web (index.wasm, index.pck, index.js present)
  - Non‑Godot web prototypes: /maze-forest and /narrative-maze
- Safely scrapped (archived, not deleted)
  - Moved to: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/aetherion-maze/_archive/20260514-…-non-godot-scrap/{maze-forest,narrative-maze}
  - Wrote manifest: MANIFEST.txt in that archive folder
- Verified live review URLs
  - http://127.0.0.1:8000/godot-web/ → 200 OK
  - /maze-forest/ and /narrative-maze/ → 404 (as intended)

What to use now
- Review the Godot build at: http://localhost:8000/godot-web/

Approvals needed

Operator update
- Advanced: Non‑Godot copies removed from live; Godot-only path confirmed healthy.
- Risk: Old bookmarks to /maze-forest or /narrative-maze will 404.
- Next: On your decision, I’ll either add redirects or leave as-is and schedule archival cleanup after a short retention window.
